quizQuestion:
結局、彼に本当のことを言え{___}だった。
(In the end, I ended up not being able to tell him the truth.)
explanation: ずじまい (ended up not doing) — 結局、彼に本当のことを言えずじまいだった。Action intended but never carried out.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
海外旅行を計画していたが、行け{___}に終わった。
(I had planned to travel abroad, but ended up not being able to go.)
explanation: ずじまい (ended up not doing) — 海外旅行を計画していたが、行けずじまいに終わった。Plans that never came to fruition.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
その本は読もうと思っていたが、読め{___}だった。
(I intended to read that book, but ended up not reading it.)
explanation: ずじまい (ended up not doing) — その本は読もうと思っていたが、読めずじまいだった。Good intentions unrealized.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
祖父に感謝の気持ちを伝えられ{___}まま、彼は亡くなった。
(He passed away while I ended up never being able to convey my gratitude to him.)
explanation: ずじまい (ended up not doing) — 祖父に感謝の気持ちを伝えられずじまいまま、彼は亡くなった。Poignant regret.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
言わ{___}、分かっているはずだ。
(Without needing to say it, you should already know.)
explanation: ずとも (without needing to/even without) — 言わずとも、分かっているはずだ。Classical/literary: the action is unnecessary.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
わざわざ来{___}、電話で十分だった。
(Without needing to come all the way here, a phone call would have been sufficient.)
explanation: ずとも (without needing to) — わざわざ来ずとも、電話で十分だった。The trip was unnecessary.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
説明せ{___}、見れば分かる。
(Without needing to explain, one can tell just by looking.)
explanation: ずとも (without needing to) — 説明せずとも、見れば分かる。Obvious enough without words.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
頼ま{___}、喜んで手伝います。
(Without needing to be asked, I will gladly help.)
explanation: ずとも (without needing to) — 頼まずとも、喜んで手伝います。Proactive willingness.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
今日は良いこと{___}の一日だった。
(Today was a day full of good things.)
explanation: ずくめ (entirely/full of) — 今日は良いことずくめの一日だった。Everything was of a single positive type.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼らは黒{___}の服装で現れた。
(They appeared dressed entirely in black.)
explanation: ずくめ (entirely/completely covered in) — 彼らは黒ずくめの服装で現れた。Uniform color context.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
最近は規制{___}で、自由が感じられない。
(Recently everything is full of regulations and one cannot feel freedom.)
explanation: ずくめ (entirely full of) — 最近は規制ずくめで、自由が感じられない。Suffocated by overwhelming restrictions.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
この一週間は嬉しいこと{___}だった。
(This past week was entirely full of happy things.)
explanation: ずくめ (entirely full of) — この一週間は嬉しいことずくめだった。A stretch of uniformly positive events.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
子供{___}解ける問題が、大人には難しい。
(A problem that even a child can solve is difficult for adults.)
explanation: すら/ですら (even/emphasis) — 子供すら解ける問題が、大人には難しい。Unexpected reversal using 'even'.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼は自分の名前{___}書けなかった。
(He could not write even his own name.)
explanation: すら/ですら (even) — 彼は自分の名前すら書けなかった。Extreme case: even the most basic thing is impossible.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
専門家{___}判断できない難しい問題だ。
(It is a difficult problem that even experts cannot judge.)
explanation: すら/ですら (even) — 専門家すら判断できない難しい問題だ。Expert inability used as an extreme marker.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
忙しくて、食事をする時間{___}ない。
(I am so busy I don't even have time to eat.)
explanation: すら/ですら (even) — 忙しくて、食事をする時間すらない。Total lack of time emphasized.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
そんな簡単なこと、できない{___}！
(I absolutely cannot do such a simple thing! (emphatic))
explanation: やしない (does not/emphatic negative) — そんな簡単なこと、できないやしない！Emphatic colloquial negation expressing frustration or certainty.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼には絶対に勝て{___}！
(There's absolutely no way I can win against him! (emphatic))
explanation: やしない (cannot/emphatic) — 彼には絶対に勝てやしない！Emphatic admission of defeat.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
あんなやつには頼め{___}。
(There's no way I can rely on someone like that. (emphatic))
explanation: やしない (cannot/emphatic negative) — あんなやつには頼めやしない。Emphatic refusal to rely on someone.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
今さら後悔したって、何も変わり{___}。
(Regretting now won't change anything. (emphatic))
explanation: やしない (will not/emphatic) — 今さら後悔したって、何も変わりやしない。Resigned emphasis that nothing will change.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
謝ろうと思っていたが、タイミングを逃して言い{___}てしまった。
(I intended to apologize but missed the moment and failed to say it.)
explanation: そびれる (fail to/miss the chance to) — 言いそびれてしまった。Missed the opportunity to do something intended.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
電車の中で声をかけようとしたが、言い出し{___}。
(I tried to speak to him on the train but failed to bring myself to say it.)
explanation: そびれる (fail to/miss chance) — 言い出しそびれた。Hesitation leading to missed opportunity.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
本を買おうと思っていたが、買い{___}まま何日も経った。
(I intended to buy the book but failed to, and several days passed.)
explanation: そびれる (fail to) — 買いそびれたまま何日も経った。Procrastination leading to failure to act.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
会議で意見を言おうとしたが、言い{___}。
(I tried to speak at the meeting but failed to say my opinion.)
explanation: そびれる (fail to) — 言いそびれた。Meeting context: unable to speak up.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
電車に乗り{___}て、会議に遅刻してしまった。
(I missed the train and ended up late for the meeting.)
explanation: 損なう/損ねる (fail to/miss) — 電車に乗り損なって、会議に遅刻してしまった。Failure to catch a train with consequences.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
一番大事なところを言い{___}て、説明が不完全になった。
(I failed to say the most important part, making the explanation incomplete.)
explanation: 損なう/損ねる (fail to) — 一番大事なところを言い損なって、説明が不完全になった。Failure to convey key information.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
絶好のチャンスを逃し{___}、悔しい思いをした。
(I failed to seize the perfect opportunity and felt frustrated.)
explanation: 損なう/損ねる (fail to/miss) — 絶好のチャンスを逃し損なって → actually: チャンスを生かし損なって. Miss opportunity through failure to act.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
上司の機嫌を損ない{___}て、気まずい雰囲気になった。
(I inadvertently offended my boss and the atmosphere became awkward.)
explanation: 損ねる (damage/offend/fail to) — 上司の機嫌を損ねて、気まずい雰囲気になった。Unintentionally spoiling someone's mood.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
嵐の中では、船を止める{___}。
(In the middle of a storm, there is no way to stop the ship.)
explanation: 術がない (no way/helpless/no means) — 嵐の中では、船を止める術がない。Complete helplessness in the face of nature.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
悪夢のような現実から逃れる{___}と思った。
(I thought there was no way to escape from the nightmare-like reality.)
explanation: 術がない (no way/helpless) — 悪夢のような現実から逃れる術がない. Trapped feeling.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼女を説得する{___}と諦めた。
(I gave up thinking there was no way to persuade her.)
explanation: 術がない (no way/helpless) — 彼女を説得する術がないと諦めた。Resignation after exhausting options.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
時間を取り戻す{___}のだから、後悔しても仕方がない。
(Since there is no way to get the time back, there's no use regretting.)
explanation: 術がない (no way) — 時間を取り戻す術がないのだから。The passage of time is irreversible.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
食事{___}、彼はすぐに仕事に戻った。
(Finishing his meal quickly, he immediately returned to work.)
explanation: もそこそこに (quickly/without completing properly/hurriedly) — 食事もそこそこに、彼はすぐに仕事に戻った。Rushed through an activity to get to what matters.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
挨拶{___}、彼女は部屋を出て行った。
(Barely finishing her greeting, she left the room.)
explanation: もそこそこに (without lingering/hurriedly) — 挨拶もそこそこに、彼女は部屋を出て行った。Abrupt departure after minimal social obligation.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
準備{___}、急いで出発した。
(Without finishing preparations properly, he departed in a hurry.)
explanation: もそこそこに (without completing/hurriedly) — 準備もそこそこに、急いで出発した。Rushed departure without adequate preparation.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
授業{___}、彼は教室を飛び出した。
(Without waiting for class to properly end, he bolted out of the classroom.)
explanation: もそこそこに (hurriedly/without completing) — 授業もそこそこに、彼は教室を飛び出した。Impatiently leaving before something ends.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼は会社員として働く{___}、小説を書いている。
(While working as a company employee, he writes novels.)
explanation: かたわら (while/alongside/in addition to) — 彼は会社員として働くかたわら、小説を書いている。Side activity alongside main occupation.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼女は主婦業の{___}、ネットショップを経営している。
(Alongside her work as a homemaker, she runs an online shop.)
explanation: かたわら (alongside/while) — 彼女は主婦業のかたわら、ネットショップを経営している。Noun + のかたわら construction.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
研究の{___}、大学で教壇に立っている。
(Alongside his research, he also teaches at university.)
explanation: かたわら (alongside/while) — 研究のかたわら、大学で教壇に立っている。Dual roles in academia.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼はスポーツ選手として活躍する{___}、慈善活動にも力を入れている。
(While active as a sports athlete, he also puts effort into charity work.)
explanation: かたわら (alongside/while) — 彼はスポーツ選手として活躍するかたわら、慈善活動にも力を入れている。Dual contribution to society.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
二人は追い{___}追われ{___}、長い間走り続けた。
(The two chased and were chased, running for a long time.)
explanation: つ～つ (alternately/back and forth, literary) — 追いつ追われつ、長い間走り続けた。Expresses alternating reciprocal actions.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
行き{___}戻り{___}、なかなか決断できなかった。
(Going back and forth, he could not make up his mind.)
explanation: つ～つ (alternately/back and forth, literary) — 行きつ戻りつ、なかなか決断できなかった。Indecision expressed through alternating movement.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
持ち{___}持たれ{___}の関係が大切だ。
(A relationship of mutual give and take is important.)
explanation: つ～つ (alternately/mutually, literary) — 持ちつ持たれつの関係が大切だ。Fixed expression for mutual support.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
波が寄せ{___}返し{___}、砂浜を洗っていた。
(Waves came and went, washing over the sandy beach.)
explanation: つ～つ (alternately, literary) — 波が寄せつ返しつ、砂浜を洗っていた。Rhythmic alternating motion in nature description.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
必ず優勝し{___}！
(I will definitely win the championship!)
explanation: てみせる (I'll definitely do it/show you I can) — 必ず優勝してみせる！Determination to prove one can do something.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
絶対にこの問題を解い{___}。
(I will definitely solve this problem and show you.)
explanation: てみせる (I'll show you I can do it) — 絶対にこの問題を解いてみせる。Confident resolve.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
諦めずに最後まで戦い抜い{___}！
(I will fight to the end without giving up and show everyone!)
explanation: てみせる (I'll definitely do it) — 諦めずに最後まで戦い抜いてみせる！Determination to persevere.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
みんなに認めさせ{___}！
(I will make everyone acknowledge me!)
explanation: てみせる (I'll show them) — みんなに認めさせてみせる！Desire to prove oneself to others.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
どこ{___}視線を向けながら、ぼんやり考えていた。
(Without looking at anything in particular, I was lost in vague thought.)
explanation: ともなく (without particularly/aimlessly) — どこともなく視線を向けながら、ぼんやり考えていた。Directionless or purposeless action.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
誰が言う{___}、その話は広まっていた。
(Without anyone particularly saying it, the story had spread.)
explanation: ともなく (without anyone in particular/aimlessly) — 誰が言うともなく、その話は広まっていた。Information spreading without clear source.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
何を見る{___}、テレビをつけっぱなしにしていた。
(Without particularly watching anything, the TV was left on.)
explanation: ともなく (without particularly/aimlessly) — 何を見るともなく、テレビをつけっぱなしにしていた。Background noise without intent.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
いつ眠る{___}、気がついたら朝になっていた。
(Without realizing when I fell asleep, before I knew it, it was morning.)
explanation: ともなく (without particularly) — いつ眠るともなく、気がついたら朝になっていた。Unconsciously drifting to sleep.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼女は涙{___}訴えた。
(She appealed while in tears.)
explanation: ながらに (while/in the state of, literary) — 彼女は涙ながらに訴えた。Emotional state maintained throughout the action.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
昔{___}の風景が、この村には残っている。
(Scenery from the old days remains in this village.)
explanation: ながらに (while/in the state of being) — 昔ながらの風景が、この村には残っている。Fixed expression: 昔ながら = old-fashioned/traditional.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
家にい{___}にして、世界中の情報を得られる時代だ。
(It is an era where you can obtain information from around the world while staying at home.)
explanation: ながらに (while/without moving from) — 家にいながらにして、世界中の情報を得られる時代だ。Modern convenience expressed with literary tone.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼は生まれ{___}にして才能に恵まれていた。
(He was blessed with talent from the moment he was born.)
explanation: ながらに (while/in the state of) — 彼は生まれながらにして才能に恵まれていた。Innate quality from birth.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
子供には子供{___}の考え方がある。
(Children have their own way of thinking.)
explanation: なりに/なりの (in one's own way/appropriate to) — 子供には子供なりの考え方がある。Each person has their own appropriate manner.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
私は私{___}に頑張っている。
(I am doing my best in my own way.)
explanation: なりに (in one's own way) — 私は私なりに頑張っている。Personal effort in one's own manner.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
彼{___}の答えが、必ずしも正解ではないかもしれない。
(His own answer may not necessarily be correct.)
explanation: なりに/なりの (in one's own way/characteristic of) — 彼なりの答えが、必ずしも正解ではないかもしれない。Acknowledging individual perspective.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

quizQuestion:
初心者{___}に工夫して、料理を完成させた。
(Devising in my own beginner's way, I completed the dish.)
explanation: なりに (in one's own way/appropriate to one's level) — 初心者なりに工夫して、料理を完成させた。Making do at one's current level.
visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion
